name: Lunar Reconnaissance Orbiter
description: Orbiting the Moon since 2009. Mapping the surface at 50 cm resolution — good enough to see the Apollo descent stages still sitting on the surface. Found water ice at the poles. Identified 200+ potential Artemis landing sites. A scout for the return.
scale_fact: The Moon is 384,400 km away — almost 1,000 times farther than the ISS. On this logarithmic map it looks close. In reality, no human has been there since 1972.
